title: Jump in Cup
result_description: You jump and disappear behind a coffee cup, and the cup splashes as if you dove inside it.

inputs.name: jump_clip
inputs.what_to_film: Act out jumping, lining your jump up exactly behind where the cup sits (use a distant ball as a center marker).

inputs.name: cup_plate
inputs.what_to_film: Clean shot of just the cup in place with you out of frame — the foreground cup to cut out.

capture_requirements.note: shoot all three in one continuous take to keep framing identical

inputs.name: splash_clip
inputs.what_to_film: A splash from/around the cup to trigger on the jump landing.

edit_steps.note: cut the jump so it ends on the frame you disappear behind the cup
start the splash on its first splash frame; jump-cut it straight after the jump
stack the clean cup clip on the layer above so it can be the foreground
smart-brush the cup (and its handle) to cut it out as a foreground object; rectangle mask is the free fallback

params.note: creator tried 2.0, settled ~1.5 to feel natural

edit_steps.note: speed up the splash so it lands closer to the jump timing; re-trim the tail after
optional seam fix: copy the clean cup clip to patch the grass that moved underfoot
feathered rectangle over the moving-grass corner so the ground stays static through the splash
add a small water-splash SFX, nudged slightly earlier than the visual splash
optional teaser text (e.g. a hook) and start the clip right before the jump to cut dead space

result.description: A clean jump-cut hides you behind the cut-out cup, then a sped-up splash sells the dive into the cup.
success_criteria: Cup reads as a foreground object over the splash
Jump and splash timing feel continuous
No moving-grass seam at the transition
